summary: The answer is that **impression plasters** display characteristics similar to those found in materials exhibiting true hysteresis through their reversible volume change upon repeated wetting-drying cycles. This contrasts sharply with irreversible hydrocolloids which do not fully revert back after being deformed permanently by external forces like pressure application alone does not induce such complex cyclic responses as seen here within these dental casting agents. To be precise, they mimic core aspects defining classical definitions around this phenomenon involving both storage & loss moduli over multiple loading-unloading sequences characteristic only when certain types of viscoelastomers are subjected under controlled conditions matching what we observe clinically from use cases reported across literature reviews consulted today about various
